message:
feat(tui): add dirty-state gated data ingestion to all TUI screens

Introduce a DataGeneration-based dirty-state tracking mechanism across
all 12 TUI screens plus tui_bridge. Each screen now stores a
`last_data_gen` snapshot and only performs expensive data ingestion,
rebuild, or refresh operations when the underlying data has actually
changed (events, db_stats, or requests).

This eliminates redundant re-queries on every tick cycle when the server
is idle, significantly reducing CPU usage in the TUI dashboard. Key
changes per screen:

- agents: gate ingest_events on dirty.events, rebuild on dirty.db_stats
- analytics: gate periodic refresh_feed on dirty.any()
- archive_browser: gate periodic tree refresh on dirty.any()
- attachments: gate interval reload on dirty.db_stats/events
- explorer: gate pressure_board refresh on dirty.db_stats
- messages: gate empty-query periodic refresh on dirty.any()
- projects: gate ingest_events/rebuild on dirty.events/db_stats
- reservations: gate db_snapshot/events/rebuild on respective dirty flags
- search: wire up dirty tracking (preparatory, not yet gated)
- system_health: add field (reserved for future gating)
- threads: gate periodic list refresh on dirty.events/db_stats
- tool_metrics: gate hydrate/ingest/rebuild on respective dirty flags
- tui_bridge: update ServerStarted test event to match new event schema
  (seq, source, redacted, endpoint, config_summary fields)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
